1.gl: to send away, for, or out (in a great variety of applications)
1.kj: [idiom] any wise, appoint, bring (on the way), cast (away, out), conduct, [idiom] earnestly, forsake, give (up), grow long, lay, leave, let depart (down, go, loose), push away, put

1.gl: a ram (as full-grown and fat), including a battering-ram (as butting); hence, a meadow (as for sheep); also a pad or camel's saddle (as puffed out)
1.kj: captain, furniture, lamb, (large) pasture, ram. See also H1033 (בֵּית כַּר), H3746 (כָּרִי).
1.dv: from H3769 (כָּרַר) in the sense of plumpness;

1.gl: a daughter (used in the same wide sense as other terms of relationship, literally and figuratively)
1.kj: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, [idiom] first, [idiom] old, [phrase] owl, town, village.

1.gl: a pasture (i.e. open field, whither cattle are driven); by implication, a desert; also speech (including its organs)
1.dv: from H1696 (דָבַר) in the sense of driving;

1.gl: the earth (at large, or partitively a land)
1.kj: [idiom] common, country, earth, field, ground, land, [idiom] natins, way, [phrase] wilderness, world.
1.dv: from an unused root probably meaning to be firm;

1.gl: Tsijon (as a permanent capital), a mountain of Jerusalem
1.dv: the same (regularly) as H6725 (צִיּוּן)lemma צִיוּן missing dagesh, corrected to צִיּוּן;

2.kj: chase (away), [idiom] could not, depart, flee ([idiom] apace, away), (re-) move, thrust away, wander (abroad, -er, -ing).

2.kj: apple (of the eye), branch, company, daughter, [idiom] first, [idiom] old, [phrase] owl, town, village.

Moab, an incestuous son of Lot; also his territory and descendants

3.kj: chase (away), [idiom] could not, depart, flee ([idiom] apace, away), (re-) move, thrust away, wander (abroad, -er, -ing).

4.gl: the face (as the part that turns); used in a great variety of applications (literally and figuratively); also (with prepositional prefix) as a preposition (before, etc.)

4.dv: plural (but always as singular) of an unused noun פָּנֶה; from H6437 (פָּנָה));

4.kj: abide, assemble, be afraid, dwell, fear, gather (together), inhabitant, remain, sojourn, stand in awe, (be) stranger, [idiom] surely.

4.kj: banish, bring, cast down (out), chase, compel, draw away, drive (away, out, quite), fetch a stroke, force, go away, outcast, thrust away (out), withdraw.

6.gl: arrogance or majesty; by implication, (concretely) ornament
6.kj: excellency, haughtiness, highness, pride, proudly, swelling.

7.gl: a particle of affirmation, surely; hence (by limitation) only
7.kj: also, in any wise, at least, but, certainly, even, howbeit, nevertheless, notwithstanding, only, save, surely, of a surety, truly, verily, [phrase] wherefore, yet (but).

7.gl: Kir-Cheres or Kir-Chareseth, a place in Moab

7.gl: to murmur (in pleasure or anger); by implication, to ponder
7.kj: imagine, meditate, mourn, mutter, roar, [idiom] sore, speak, study, talk, utter.

7.gl: Moab, an incestuous son of Lot; also his territory and descendants

from the same as H784 (אֵשׁ) (in the sense of pressing down firmly; compare H803 (אֲשׁוּיָה));

8.gl: properly, to touch, i.e. lay the hand upon (for any purpose; euphemistically, to lie with a woman); by implication, to reach (figuratively, to arrive, acquire); violently, to strike (punish, defeat, destroy, etc.)
8.kj: beat, ([idiom] be able to) bring (down), cast, come (nigh), draw near (nigh), get up, happen, join, near, plague, reach (up), smite, strike, touch.

8.gl: properly, to pound, i.e. smite; by implication (as if beating out, and thus expanding) to disperse; also, to thrust off, down, out or upon (inclusively, reject, let alone, permit, remit, etc.)
8.kj: cast off, drawn, let fall, forsake, join (battle), leave (off), lie still, loose, spread (self) abroad, stretch out, suffer.

8.gl: a sea (as breaking in noisy surf) or large body of water; specifically (with the article), the Mediterranean Sea; sometimes a large river, or an artifical basin; locally, the west, or (rarely) the south
8.dv: from an unused root meaning to roar;

8.gl: to vacillate, i.e. reel or stray (literally or figuratively); also causative of both
8.kj: (cause to) go astray, deceive, dissemble, (cause to, make to) err, pant, seduce, (make to) stagger, (cause to) wander, be out of the way.

9.gl: severed, i.e. harvest (as reaped), the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb (of a tree, or simply foliage)
to slake the thirst (occasionally of other appetites)
9.kj: bathe, make drunk, (take the) fill, satiate, (abundantly) satisfy, soak, water (abundantly).
[idiom] at all, bewail, complain, make lamentation, [idiom] more, mourn, [idiom] sore, [idiom] with tears, weep.

9.gl: Elale or Elaleh, a place east of the Jordan

9.gl: harvest (as the crop), whether the product (grain or fruit) or the (dry) season

10.gl: to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. remove (destroy, leave behind, put up, restore, etc.)
10.kj: assemble, bring, consume, destroy, felch, gather (in, together, up again), [idiom] generally, get (him), lose, put all together, receive, recover (another from leprosy), (be) rerew

10.gl: to repose, i.e. desist from exertion; used in many implied relations (causative, figurative or specific)
10.kj: (cause to, let, make to) cease, celebrate, cause (make) to fail, keep (sabbath), suffer to be lacking, leave, put away (down), (make to) rest, rid, still, take away.

10.gl: to mar (especially by breaking); figuratively, to split the ears (with sound), i.e. shout (for alarm or joy)
10.kj: blow an alarm, cry (alarm, aloud, out), destroy, make a joyful noise, smart, shout (for joy), sound an alarm, triumph.

10.gl: properly, to creak (or emit a stridulous sound), i.e. to shout (usually for joy)
10.kj: aloud for joy, cry out, be joyful (greatly, make to) rejoice, (cause to) shout (for joy), (cause to) sing (aloud, for joy, out), triumph.

10.gl: a trough (as dug out); specifically, a wine-vat (whether the lower one, into which the juice drains; or the upper, in which the grapes are crushed)
10.dv: from an unused root meaning to excavate;

10.gl: to tread; by implication, to walk; also to string abow (by treading on it in bending)
10.kj: archer, bend, come, draw, go (over), guide, lead (forth), thresh, tread (down), walk.
